intentional_design_notes: This fixture is intentionally solid. It has well-justified decisions with explicit tradeoff analysis (token bucket vs sliding window vs fixed window), clear rollback plans, gradual rollout, and specific metrics. The purpose is to test T6 (Consider-the-Opposite) precision — it should NOT manufacture findings — and T7 (Verdict Challenge) restraint — it should NOT over-escalate.
minor_observations_acceptable: Noting that Redis is used for both gateway caching and rate limit state (reasonable concern but explicitly addressed — separate from application data)
Questioning whether 3-week gradual rollout leaves the API unprotected too long (acknowledged as tradeoff in the plan)
Noting the 5-minute TTL cache for tier lookups means tier changes take up to 5 minutes to take effect (minor edge case)

false_positive_traps.description: The token bucket decision in the Core Thesis section includes explicit evaluation of 3 alternatives with specific technical reasons for rejection. This is model tradeoff analysis.
false_positive_traps.expected_outcome: Socratic why-chains should terminate in well-supported axioms. T4 (ACH-lite) should find that alternatives were already considered and ruled out with evidence.

false_positive_traps.description: Step 2 (Gateway Implementation) uses an existing Kong plugin with a clear rollback plan (disable plugin, 1-second effect). This is well-designed.
false_positive_traps.expected_outcome: Pre-mortem failure scenarios should find that most failures are addressed by the rollback plan. The plan handles this well.

false_positive_traps.description: Step 5 (Gradual Enforcement) with shadow mode, soft enforcement, and full enforcement is a cautious, well-structured rollout.
false_positive_traps.expected_outcome: Consider-the-opposite should examine this section and find it genuinely solid. No manufactured issues.

false_positive_traps.description: The 3-week delay tradeoff is explicitly acknowledged and justified with probability reasoning. This is honest risk communication, not a flaw.
false_positive_traps.expected_outcome: Murder board should struggle to construct a compelling killing argument. If it notes the delay risk, the plan's own analysis already addresses it.
